title: 8 of the Best Herbal Creams for Plantar Fasciitis Pain Management

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for plantar fasciitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area.
Tannins are good for plantar fasciitis because they help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the affected area, promoting faster healing and pain relief for the plantar fascia.
Polyphenols are good for plantar fasciitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling in the feet, allowing for faster healing of the plantar fascia.
Sesquiterpenes are good for plantar fasciitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area of the foot.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana cream is a natural remedy that can help with plantar fasciitis pain. This cream is made from the flowers of the arnica plant, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you apply the cream to your affected foot, the arnica's active ingredients, such as sesquiterpene lactones, work to reduce swelling and pain in the plantar fascia, the band of tissue that runs along the bottom of your foot. This reduces tension and pressure on the tissue, giving you relief from sharp pains when you walk or stand. Arnica montana cream also improves blood flow to the area, which helps to promote healing and reduce pain and swelling. This natural remedy can be used in addition to other treatments, such as stretching and physical therapy, to help manage plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal creams that help with plantar fasciitis, start by harvesting or purchasing dried Calendula flowers. Begin by steeping 1 tablespoon of the dried flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for about 5-7 minutes to make a tea. Let the mixture cool and then strain it to get the liquid. Next, mix 1/4 cup of coconut oil or other carrier oil with 2 tablespoons of the cooled tea in a small bowl. Add a few drops of vitamin E oil and a teaspoon of beeswax to the mixture, and stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved. Transfer the mixture to a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture occasionally as it warms up, until it reaches a temperature of around 120-130°F. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken before transferring it to a storage container. Once the cream is set, apply it to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal creams for plantar fasciitis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for plantar fasciitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the plantar fascia by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Aloin is good for plantar fasciitis because it reduces inflammation and pain by stopping the growth of new blood vessels that cause swelling in the affected area.
Polysaccharides are good for plantar fasciitis because they help reduce inflammation and promote healing by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Aloe-emodin is good for plantar fasciitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, making it easier to move and exercise.
Glycoproteins are good for plantar fasciitis because they help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, relieving pain and discomfort caused by the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream can help with plantar fasciitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When the plantar fascia, a band of tissue in your foot, gets inflamed, it can be painful. Aloe barbadensis cream can reduce this inflammation. The cream also has cooling properties that can ease the pain and discomfort.  Plantar fasciitis often causes pain on the bottom of the foot, especially when you walk or stand. This pain can be sharp or dull, and it can be hard to put weight on your foot. Aloe barbadensis cream can help to reduce this pain and make it easier to walk or stand. It can also help to reduce swelling and redness in the affected area. Using Aloe barbadensis cream on the affected area can also help to reduce the risk of further injury. This is because the cream can help to protect the plantar fascia from further irritation and stress. Additionally, the cream can help to promote healing in the affected area. This can help to repair any damage to the plantar fascia and reduce the risk of future episodes of plantar fasciitis. Aloe barbadensis cream is also gentle on the skin and can be used as often as needed. This makes it a convenient and effective treatment option for plantar fasciitis. The cream can be applied directly to the affected area after a warm bath or shower to help it absorb better into the skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal creams that help with plantar fasciitis, you'll need dried Calendula flowers and a few basic ingredients. First, infuse the dried flowers in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process helps the beneficial properties of the Calendula to mix with the oil. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter, and add some essential oils like peppermint or eucalyptus for their anti-inflammatory properties. Finally, heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax or shea butter has melted and the mixture has thickened to a smooth, creamy texture. Let the mixture cool and harden, then transfer it to a clean container and apply it to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for plantar fasciitis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for plantar fasciitis because it reduces inflammation and pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause swelling and discomfort in the affected area.

Flavonoids are good for plantar fasciitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the affected area, which can help alleviate the discomfort and stiffness associated with this condition.
Polyphenols are good for plantar fasciitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the foot, making them a natural remedy for soothing plantar fasciitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams are often used to help people with plantar fasciitis. This is because the cream contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that reduces inflammation and swelling. Plantar fasciitis is a condition where the band of tissue on the bottom of the foot becomes inflamed, causing pain and discomfort. The glycyrrhizin in the cream works by blocking the production of a chemical that causes swelling, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. The cream also has anti-oxidant properties, which help to protect the tissue from further damage and promote healing. When applied to the affected area, the cream can help to reduce pain and inflammation, making it easier to move and walk without discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Calendula officinalis herbal cream for plantar fasciitis, you will need dried Calendula flowers, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other common ingredients. First, infuse the dried Calendula flowers in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting them sit in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in the microwave in short intervals, being careful not to burn it. Once the beeswax is fully melted, slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax, stirring constantly to combine. Then, add a few drops of vitamin E oil and a pinch of sea salt to enhance the cream's anti-inflammatory properties. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before applying it to the affected area of your foot to help reduce pain and inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for plantar fasciitis

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa cream, also known as turmeric cream, is a natural remedy that can help ease plantar fasciitis pain. The active ingredient, curcumin, is a powerful anti-inflammatory that reduces swelling and pain in the affected area. When applied topically, curcumin cream helps to decrease inflammation in the plantar fascia, the band of tissue that runs along the bottom of the foot. By reducing inflammation, the cream alleviates pain and discomfort in the heel and foot. Turmeric cream also contains antioxidants that help to repair damaged tissue and promote healing. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce stiffness and improve mobility in the foot, making it easier to walk and engage in daily activities. Applying curcumin cream to the affected area may help to alleviate pain and promote healing, making it a potential natural remedy for plantar fasciitis sufferers.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal cream for plantar fasciitis, start by infusing dried calendula flowers in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Fill a clean glass jar with a handful of dried calendula flowers, and then pour the carrier oil over the flowers, making sure they are completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process allows the beneficial properties of the calendula flowers to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the calendula flowers. Next, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a creamy texture. Finally, add a few drops of a natural emulsifier like vitamin E oil to help the cream blend smoothly. Once the cream is fully mixed, pour it into small containers and let it cool before use. Apply a small amount of the calendula cream to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation and promote healing of plantar fasciitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help with plantar fasciitis pain. The creams made from ginger contain compounds that reduce inflammation and ease pain. When applied to the affected area, these creams help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the heel and bottom of the foot. Ginger also has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the irritated plantar fascia, the band of tissue that supports the arch of the foot. By reducing inflammation and pain, ginger creams can help people with plantar fasciitis to move around and walk more comfortably. Additionally, ginger has natural anti-oxidant properties that can help to prevent further damage to the plantar fascia, making it a useful addition to a treatment plan.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Calendula officinalis herbal cream that can help with plantar fasciitis, you will need dried calendula flowers, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other natural ingredients. First, steep one tablespoon of dried calendula flowers in two tablespoons of carrier oil in a small saucepan over low heat for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their medicinal properties into the oil. Next, strain the mixture and discard the flowers. In a separate saucepan, melt two tablespoons of beeswax and one tablespoon of shea butter over low heat, stirring occasionally. Once the beeswax and shea butter are melted, slowly add the calendula oil mixture to the saucepan and stir well. Continue to heat the mixture for another 5-10 minutes, until it thickens and forms a smooth, creamy consistency. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly. Pour the mixture into a clean container, such as a glass jar, and let it cool completely before use. This calendula cream can be applied directly to the affected area of the foot, such as the heel or arch, to help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for plantar fasciitis

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is a plant that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The cream made from its flowers is often recommended for plantar fasciitis, a condition that causes pain in the heel and bottom of the foot. The cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. It also has antiseptic properties, which help prevent infection and promote healing. When applied to the foot, the cream can help relax the plantar fascia, a band of tissue that runs along the bottom of the foot, and reduce tension. By reducing pain and inflammation, the cream can make it easier to move and perform daily activities without discomfort. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum cream is often used to improve circulation and reduce scarring, which can also help alleviate plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal creams that help with plantar fasciitis, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Calendula flowers, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and essential oils such as peppermint or eucalyptus. Begin by infusing the dried Calendula flowers in a carrier oil like coconut oil for several weeks. This will help release the medicinal properties of the flowers into the oil. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil and discard the flowers. In a double boiler, melt the beeswax and shea butter, then add the infused oil and stir until well combined. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add a few drops of peppermint or eucalyptus essential oil for their anti-inflammatory properties and to help ease pain. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely before use. To apply, gently massage a small amount of the cream onto the affected area, ideally after a warm bath or shower, to help reduce inflammation and promote healing of the plantar fascia.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana, also known as witch hazel, is a plant that has been used for a long time to help with pain and swelling. The cream made from this plant contains ingredients that can help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the feet, which is common in plantar fasciitis. When you apply the cream to the affected area, the anti-inflammatory properties in witch hazel can help calm down the pain and swelling in the plantar fascia, a band of tissue that runs along the bottom of your foot. The cream can also help reduce redness and soreness, making it easier to walk and move around. Additionally, witch hazel has antiseptic properties, which can help prevent infection in the area.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal creams for plantar fasciitis relief, start by harvesting the flowers of the Calendula plant and drying them thoroughly in a cool, dry place. Once dried, grind the flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine 2 tablespoons of the Calendula powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Heat the mixture, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the ingredients are fully incorporated. Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool slightly before adding 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 2 drops of peppermint essential oil. Stir well to combine and pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container. Let the mixture cool and solidify before using it as a topical cream to soothe and calm plantar fasciitis pain and inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with plantar fasciitis. This condition causes pain and inflammation in the heel of the foot. The cream, made from the flowers of the marigold plant, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The antioxidants in the cream can also help to protect the tissue from damage and promote healing. When applied to the heel, the cream can soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce the tension in the plantar fascia, a band of tissue that runs along the bottom of the foot and can become irritated and inflamed in people with plantar fasciitis. Regular use of Calendula officinalis cream may help to reduce pain and inflammation, making it easier to move and walk without discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal cream that can help with plantar fasciitis, you will first need to infuse the dried flowers of the Calendula plant in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil. To do this, combine 1 cup of dried Calendula flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar, and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This will allow the active compounds in the Calendula to dissolve into the oil, creating a soothing and anti-inflammatory infusion. Once the infusion is complete, you will need to strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Next, you will add a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, to the infused oil to create a rich and spreadable cream. This can be done by melting the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler, then slowly adding the infused oil while stirring constantly. Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, you can transfer it to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area of the foot, providing relief from the pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for plantar fasciitis

intro_desc: Plantar fasciitis is a painful condition that affects the heel and bottom of the foot. It's caused by inflammation of the plantar fascia, a band of tissue that supports the arch of the foot. Using herbal creams can help alleviate this pain. One such herb is Arnica montana, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease discomfort. When applied topically, Arnica can penetrate deep into the skin, providing quick relief from pain and inflammation. Another herb, Aloe barbadensis, is also beneficial in treating plantar fasciitis. Aloe vera contains compounds that soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. By applying a cream containing these herbs, you can reduce the pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis.  Treating plantar fasciitis is essential because if left untreated, it can lead to chronic pain and limited mobility. This can make everyday activities like walking, standing, and even sleeping uncomfortable. Untreated plantar fasciitis can also lead to further complications, such as developing other foot problems like heel spurs or Achilles tendonitis. By using herbal creams to alleviate the pain and inflammation, you can improve your quality of life. You can walk, stand, and engage in activities without pain, and even sleep better at night. This can also help prevent further complications and allow you to maintain an active lifestyle.
supplementary_best: A combination of Arnica montana, Curcuma longa, and Hamamelis virginiana herbal creams is effective in treating plantar fasciitis. Arnica montana cream reduces inflammation and pain by blocking the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body. Curcuma longa cream, also known as turmeric, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected area. Hamamelis virginiana cream, which contains witch hazel, helps to reduce swelling and ease pain by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation. This combination works best because it targets multiple aspects of plantar fasciitis, including pain, inflammation, and swelling. The anti-inflammatory properties of Arnica montana and Curcuma longa complement the vasoconstrictive properties of Hamamelis virginiana, providing a comprehensive approach to treating plantar fasciitis.
supplementary_avoid: If you have plantar fasciitis, there are some herbal creams you should avoid using. One such herb is 'Capsicum annuum', also known as cayenne pepper. This herb contains compounds that can increase blood flow and reduce pain, but it can also irritate the skin and make plantar fasciitis symptoms worse. Another herb to avoid is 'Eucalyptus globulus', which has anti-inflammatory properties but can be too harsh for sensitive skin. 'Tea tree oil', derived from 'Melaleuca alternifolia', can be irritating to the skin and cause allergic reactions in some people, which may make plantar fasciitis symptoms worse. 'Thymus vulgaris', or thyme, can also be irritating to the skin and cause allergic reactions, similar to 'Melaleuca alternifolia'. Finally, 'Salix alba', or white willow, can be too astringent and dry out the skin, which may make plantar fasciitis symptoms worse. It's best to avoid using creams containing these herbs, especially if you have sensitive skin.
supplementary_other_remedies: Epsom salt baths or foot soaks can help with plantar fasciitis by reducing swelling and relaxing the muscles in your foot. The magnesium in Epsom salt helps to ease pain and inflammation, which can make it easier to move your foot and do everyday activities. Cold or warm compresses can also be used to help relieve pain and reduce swelling. Applying ice to the affected area can help numb the pain, while a warm compress can help loosen up tight muscles. Another natural remedy is to use a foam roller or a tennis ball to roll out the bottom of your foot, which can help break up scar tissue and adhesions that can contribute to plantar fasciitis. Additionally, using a supportive arch or insole in your shoe can help redistribute pressure and take some of the stress off of the plantar fascia.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with plantar fasciitis often experience other foot problems like heel spurs and bunions. This is because plantar fasciitis is a condition that affects the band of tissue that connects the heel bone to the toes, causing pain and stiffness in the foot. Some medicinal herbs that may help alleviate these symptoms include turmeric, which contains a compound called curcumin that reduces inflammation and pain. Ginger is another herb that may help, as it has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease pain and swelling in the foot. Willow bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, may also be helpful in reducing pain and inflammation. St. John's Wort may also be beneficial, as it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease pain and stiffness in the foot.  Cayenne pepper, which contains capsaicin, can also help alleviate pain by reducing inflammation and blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body. Epsom salt, which is rich in magnesium, can help reduce pain and inflammation by relaxing the muscles and improving blood flow to the affected area.  In addition to foot problems, people with plantar fasciitis may also experience knee pain, ankle pain, and lower back pain, due to the way they walk and the strain this puts on their joints. These conditions can be alleviated with medicinal herbs like willow bark, which can reduce pain and inflammation, and turmeric, which can reduce pain and inflammation and improve joint mobility.  Some people with plantar fasciitis may also experience fatigue, due to the pain and stiffness in their foot, which can make it difficult to walk and perform daily activities. Medicinal herbs like ashwagandha, which is an adaptogenic herb that can help reduce stress and fatigue, and ginseng, which can improve energy and reduce fatigue, may be helpful in alleviating these symptoms.
